title: CEM Extensions for Design Systems
description: Extensions to the Custom Elements Manifest (CEM) spec for design system lifecycle management. These fields are added alongside standard CEM fields on declarations, attributes, members, slots, cssProperties, cssParts, and events.

LifecycleFields.description: Fields that can be added to any CEM declaration or member to express lifecycle state.

status.description: Component lifecycle status. 'draft' = not yet stable, 'ready' = stable for production, 'deprecated' = scheduled for removal.

removal.description: When the deprecated item will be removed. ISO date (2026-07-30), semver (4.0.0), or quarter (Q3-2026).

replacement.description: Machine-readable replacement identifier. For attributes: new attribute name or instruction. For values: the replacement value.

DeprecatedValue.description: Deprecation info for a specific attribute value (without deprecating the entire attribute).

removal.description: When this value will be removed

AttributeExtensions.description: Extension fields for CEM attribute/member entries.

removal.description: When the deprecated attribute will be removed

replacement.description: What to use instead of this attribute

deprecatedValues.description: Individual deprecated values. When present, the attribute itself is NOT deprecated — only these specific values are.

SlotExtensions.description: Extension fields for CEM slot entries.

CssPropertyExtensions.description: Extension fields for CEM cssProperty entries.
